key_insight: A dual-continuum phase-field model separates fracture-flow pressure from matrix pore pressure, enabling hydraulic-fracture simulations that can capture viscosity-dominated pressure drop and fluid lag.
engineering_relevance: This improves fidelity for high-rate subsurface injection scenarios where conventional smeared-pressure phase-field models miss fracture-front lag and transient flow behavior.
method: Separate mesoscale crack pressure and micropore pressure in a variational framework
Modified fixed-stress split plus variational inequality to stabilize coupling and enforce fluid lag
evidence_signal: The model is verified against closed-form solutions in toughness-dominated, viscosity-dominated, and early-time transitional regimes and reproduces transient fluid lag.
watch_item: The added two-pressure coupling and inequality constraint may increase implementation complexity and require careful numerical tuning for stability.
paper_title: A dual--continuum phase-field model for hydraulic fracturing: Viscosity-dominated regime and fluid lag
